session.user:
Find the following in the codebase and report back their signatures/implementations:
1. `find_worktree_for_branch` method - look in the worktree ABC, real, and fake implementations. I need to know: - The method signature (parameters and return type) - The fake implementation (how to set up test data)
2. `WorktreeInfo` dataclass - its fields
3. The `FakeGit` constructor - specifically how `worktrees` parameter works
4. `build_workspace_test_context` function signature
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Report exact file paths and line numbers.
session.assistant: I'll help you find these items in the codebase. Let me start by checking the documentation as instructed.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)
... [22 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Worktree Metadata Storage 3→read_when: 4→ - "storing per-worktree data" 5→ - "working with worktrees.toml" 6→ - "associating metadata with worktrees" 7→ - "implementing subdirectory navigation" 8→ - "preserving relative path on worktree switch" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Worktree Metadata Storage 12→ 13→## Overview 14→ 15→Per-worktree metadata is stored in `~/.erk/repos/{repo}/worktrees.toml`. This file associates worktree names with metadata. 16→ 17→## File Location 18→ 19→``` 20→~/.erk/repos/ 21→└── {repo-name}/ 22→ ├── config.toml ← Repo-level configuration 23→ └── worktrees.toml ← Per-worktree metadata 24→``` 25→ 26→## API 27→ 28→**Utilities**: `src/erk/core/worktree_utils.py` — contains worktree lookup and navigation helpers (`find_current_worktree`, `compute_relative_path_in_worktree`, etc.). 29→ 30→## Subdirectory Navigation Patterns
... [30 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Erk Test Reference 3→last_audited: "2026-02-13 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "writing tests for erk" 7→ - "using erk fakes" 8→ - "running erk test commands" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "modifying business logic in src/ without adding a test" 11→ warning: "Bug fixes require regression tests (fails before, passes after). Features require behavior tests." 12→ - action: "implementing interactive prompts with ctx.console.confirm()" 13→ warning: "Ensure FakeConsole in test fixture is configured with `confirm_responses` parameter. Array length must match prompt count exactly — too few causes IndexError, too many indicates a removed prompt. See tests/commands/submit/test_existing_branch_detection.py for examples." 14→ - action: "accessing FakeGit properties in tests" 15→ warning: "FakeGit has top-level properties (e.g., `git.staged_files`, `git.deleted_branches`, `git.added_worktrees`). Worktree operations delegate to an internal FakeWorktree sub-gateway." 16→ - action: "asserting on fake-specific properties in tests using `build_workspace_test_context` with `use_graphite=True`" 17→ warning: "Production wrappers (e.g., `GraphiteBranchManager`) do not expose fake tracking properties like `submitted_branches`. Assert on observable behavior (CLI output, return values) instead of accessing fake internals through the wrapper." 18→ - action: "asserting on YAML metadata field values with exact string matching" 19→ warning: 'Assert on key-only format (''field_name:''), not ''field_name: "value"''. YAML serialization differs from Python repr.' 20→ - action: "changing cleanup or deletion behavior without updating test assertions" 21→ warning: "When behavior changes from 'delete X' to 'preserve X', update test assertions to verify the new behavior (e.g., assert file persists instead of asserting it was deleted). Stale assertions silently validate the old behavior." 22→ score: 4 23→ - action: "asserting user_output() content against capsys stdout" 24→ warning: "user_output() routes to stderr. When testing code that calls user_output(), assert against capsys.readouterr().err, not .out." 25→ - action: "using context_for_test() with wrong parameter name for issues" 26→ warning: "erk-shared uses github_issues= parameter, src/erk uses issues= parameter. These are NOT interchangeable — using wrong name causes TypeError." 27→--- 28→ 29→# Erk Test Reference 30→
... [534 lines omitted] ...
154→**If you see path mismatch errors:** Ensure FakeGit's path resolution methods are being used (they handle symlinks), not direct dict lookups. 156→#### FakeWorktree Error Injection 158→FakeWorktree (in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/worktree/fake.py`) uses string-based error injection via constructor parameters. Errors raise `RuntimeError`: 163→# Inject error for add_worktree 165→ add_worktree_error="Worktree already exists", 166→ remove_worktree_error="Worktree is locked", 170→When `add_worktree_error` or `remove_worktree_error` is set, the corresponding method raises `RuntimeError` with that message. Error injection is checked FIRST in the method body, before any success logic executes. 172→You can also inject these errors via the top-level `FakeGit` constructor: 176→ add_worktree_error="Worktree already exists", 177→ remove_worktree_error="Worktree is locked", 199→This fake supports error injection via the `create_branch_error` constructor parameter, which accepts a `BranchAlreadyExists` instance: 209→# With error injection 210→fake_with_error = FakeGitBranchOps( 226→### Common Mistake: Sentinel Path Errors 446→fake_console.assert_not_contains("Error") 449→### Error Behavior 451→If `confirm()` is called but no responses remain, FakeConsole raises `AssertionError` with the prompt text. This catches missing test setup. 506→When refactoring gateway methods from exception-based to discriminated unions, update test names to reflect the new pattern. 508→### Pattern: Exception -> Union 510→| Old Test Name (Exception-Based) | New Test Name (Discriminated Union) | 512→| `test_merge_pr_failure_returns_false` | `test_merge_pr_returns_merge_error` | 515→| `test_get_issue_raises_api_error` | `test_get_issue_returns_api_error` | 524→**Error Case:** 526→- Old: `test_<method>_raises_<error>` or `test_<method>_returns_false` 527→- New: `test_<method>_returns_<error_type>` 535→3. [ ] Update docstring to describe return type (not exception) 536→4. [ ] Verify fake setup returns type (not raises exception) 541→When function signatures change (e.g., adding a column to a table, adding a new parameter), **all test fixtures must be updated comprehensively**. Partial updates cause hard-to-debug failures.

1→--- 2→title: Test Context Composition 3→read_when: 4→ - "using build_workspace_test_context with custom fakes" 5→ - "debugging invisible metadata writes in tests" 6→ - "understanding issues_explicitly_passed flag" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating custom FakeGitHubIssues without passing to test context builder" 9→ warning: "Always pass issues=issues to build_workspace_test_context when using custom FakeGitHubIssues. Without it, plan_backend operates on a different instance and metadata writes are invisible." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Test Context Composition 13→ 14→When building test contexts with custom fakes, the composition matters — particularly for `FakeGitHubIssues` instances that are shared across multiple consumers. 15→ 16→## The Shared Instance Problem 17→ 18→`build_workspace_test_context()` creates a default `FakeGitHubIssues` if none is passed. If you create your own `FakeGitHubIssues` but don't pass it to the context builder, two separate instances exist: 19→ 20→1. Your instance (which you assert against) 21→2. The context builder's internal instance (which `plan_backend` uses) 22→ 23→Metadata writes through `plan_backend` go to instance #2, invisible to your assertions on instance #1. 24→ 25→## Correct Pattern 26→ 27→```python 28→def test_metadata_is_written(env: ErkInMemEnv) -> None: 29→ issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: IssueInfo( 30→ number=42, title="Plan", body="# Plan\n\nContent", ...
... [28 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Abstract interface for git worktree operations. 2→ 3→This module provides a clean abstraction over git worktree subprocess calls, 4→making the codebase more testable and maintainable. 5→ 6→Architecture: 7→- Worktree: Abstract base class defining the interface 8→- RealWorktree: Production implementation using subprocess 9→- FakeWorktree: In-memory implementation for testing 10→""" 11→ 12→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 13→from pathlib import Path 14→ 15→# Re-export WorktreeInfo from the main git.abc module for backwards compatibility 16→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 17→ 18→ 19→class Worktree(ABC): 20→ """Abstract interface for git worktree operations. 21→ 22→ All implementations (real and fake) must implement this interface. 23→ """ 24→ 25→ @abstractmethod 26→ def list_worktrees(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[WorktreeInfo]: 27→ """List all worktrees in the repository.""" 28→ ... 29→ 30→ @abstractmethod
... [141 lines omitted] ...
50→ RuntimeError: If the worktree add operation fails. 69→ RuntimeError: If the worktree remove operation fails.

1→"""Fake worktree operations for testing. 2→ 3→FakeWorktree is an in-memory implementation that accepts pre-configured state 4→in its constructor. Construct instances directly with keyword arguments. 5→""" 6→ 7→import os 8→from pathlib import Path 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 11→from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 12→ 13→ 14→class FakeWorktree(Worktree): 15→ """In-memory fake implementation of git worktree operations. 16→ 17→ State Management: 18→ ----------------- 19→ This fake maintains mutable state to simulate git's stateful behavior. 20→ Operations like add_worktree, move_worktree modify internal state. 21→ State changes are visible to subsequent method calls within the same test. 22→ 23→ Constructor Injection: 24→ --------------------- 25→ All INITIAL state is provided via constructor (immutable after construction). 26→ Runtime mutations occur through operation methods. 27→ Tests should construct fakes with complete initial state. 28→ 29→ Mutation Tracking: 30→ -----------------
... [272 lines omitted] ...
43→ add_worktree_error: str | None = None, 44→ remove_worktree_error: str | None = None, 52→ add_worktree_error: If set, add_worktree raises RuntimeError with this message 53→ remove_worktree_error: Error message to raise from remove_worktree() for error injection 60→ self._add_worktree_error = add_worktree_error 61→ self._remove_worktree_error = remove_worktree_error 102→ Raises RuntimeError if add_worktree_error is configured. 104→ if self._add_worktree_error is not None: 105→ raise RuntimeError(self._add_worktree_error) 133→ # Check for error injection first 134→ if self._remove_worktree_error is not None: 135→ raise RuntimeError(self._remove_worktree_error) 188→ except ImportError: 217→ except (ValueError, OSError, RuntimeError): 218→ # Not relative to this existing_path or error checking, continue 228→ except (OSError, RuntimeError):

1→"""Production worktree implementation using subprocess. 2→ 3→This module provides the real worktree implementation that executes actual git 4→worktree commands via subprocess. 5→""" 6→ 7→import os 8→import subprocess 9→from pathlib import Path 10→ 11→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 12→from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 13→from erk_shared.subprocess_utils import run_subprocess_with_context 14→ 15→ 16→class RealWorktree(Worktree): 17→ """Production implementation using subprocess. 18→ 19→ All worktree operations execute actual git commands via subprocess. 20→ """ 21→ 22→ def list_worktrees(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[WorktreeInfo]: 23→ """List all worktrees in the repository.""" 24→ result = run_subprocess_with_context( 25→ cmd=["git", "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"], 26→ operation_context="list worktrees", 27→ cwd=repo_root, 28→ ) 29→ 30→ worktrees: list[WorktreeInfo] = []

174→ # Exit code 0 means no changes, non-zero means changes exist or error

1→"""High-level git operations interface. 2→ 3→This module provides a clean abstraction over git subprocess calls, making the 4→codebase more testable and maintainable. 5→ 6→Architecture: 7→- Git: Abstract base class defining the interface 8→- RealGit: Production implementation using subprocess 9→- Standalone functions: Convenience wrappers delegating to module singleton 10→""" 11→ 12→from __future__ import annotations 13→ 14→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from pathlib import Path 17→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, NamedTuple 18→ 19→if TYPE_CHECKING: 20→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 21→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.abc import GitBranchOps 22→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.abc import GitCommitOps 23→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.abc import GitConfigOps 24→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.abc import GitRebaseOps 25→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 26→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.abc import GitRepoOps 27→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.abc import GitStatusOps 28→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.abc import GitTagOps 29→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 30→

session.tool_result:
200→ dirty_worktrees: Set of worktree paths that have uncommitted/staged/untracked changes 201→ branch_last_commit_times: Mapping of (repo_root, branch, trunk) -> ISO 8601 timestamp 202→ repository_roots: Mapping of cwd -> repository root path 203→ diff_to_branch: Mapping of (cwd, branch) -> diff output 204→ merge_conflicts: Mapping of (base_branch, head_branch) -> has conflicts bool 205→ commits_ahead: Mapping of (cwd, base_branch) -> commit count 206→ commits_behind: Mapping of (cwd, target_branch) -> commit count 207→ remote_urls: Mapping of (repo_root, remote_name) -> remote URL 208→ ref_file_contents: Mapping of (ref, file_path) -> bytes for read_file_from_ref 209→ add_all_raises: Exception to raise when add_all() is called 210→ fetch_branch_raises: Exception to raise when fetch_branch() is called 211→ pull_branch_raises: Exception to raise when pull_branch() is called 212→ conflicted_files: List of file paths with merge conflicts 213→ rebase_in_progress: Whether a rebase is currently in progress. 214→ Can be a bool or a callable(cwd) -> bool for dynamic behavior. 215→ rebase_continue_raises: Exception to raise when rebase_continue() is called 216→ rebase_continue_clears_rebase: If True, rebase_continue() clears the rebase state 217→ commit_messages_since: Mapping of (cwd, base_branch) -> list of commit messages 218→ head_commit_messages_full: Mapping of cwd -> full commit message for HEAD 219→ git_user_name: Configured git user.name to return from get_git_user_name() 220→ branch_commits_with_authors: Mapping of (repo_root, branch, trunk, limit) 221→ -> commit dicts with keys: sha, author, timestamp 222→ push_to_remote_error: PushError to return when push_to_remote() is called 223→ existing_tags: Set of tag names that exist in the repository 224→ branch_divergence: Mapping of (cwd, branch, remote) -> BranchDivergence 225→ for is_branch_diverged_from_remote() 226→ rebase_onto_result: Result to return from rebase_onto(). Defaults to success. 227→ rebase_abort_raises: Exception to raise when rebase_abort() is called 228→ pull_rebase_error: PullRebaseError to return when pull_rebase() is called 229→ merge_bases: Mapping of (ref1, ref2) -> merge base commit SHA for

session.assistant:
Perfect! I have all the information needed. Let me compile my findings:
## Summary
I've found all the requested items in the codebase. Here are the exact signatures, implementations, and file locations:
---
### 1. `find_worktree_for_branch` Method
**ABC Signature** (abstract interface): - **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/worktree/abc.py` - **Lines**: 79-89 ```python @abstractmethod def find_worktree_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> Path | None: """Find worktree path for given branch name.
Args: repo_root: Repository root path branch: Branch name to search for
Returns: Path to worktree if branch is checked out, None otherwise """ ... ```
**Real Implementation** (`RealWorktree`): - **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/worktree/real.py` - **Lines**: 149-155 ```python def find_worktree_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> Path | None: """Find worktree path for given branch name.""" worktrees = self.list_worktrees(repo_root) for wt in worktrees: if wt.branch == branch: return wt.path return None ```
**Fake Implementation** (`FakeWorktree`): - **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/worktree/fake.py` - **Lines**: 150-156 ```python def find_worktree_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> Path | None: """Find worktree path for given branch name in fake data.""" worktrees = self.list_worktrees(repo_root) for wt in worktrees: if wt.branch == branch: return wt.path return None ```
**How to Set Up Test Data (FakeWorktree)**: ```python from pathlib import Path from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.fake import FakeWorktree
# Create FakeWorktree with initial worktrees repo_root = Path("/repo") wt_feature = Path("/repo/wt-feature")
fake_worktree = FakeWorktree( worktrees={ repo_root: [ WorktreeInfo(path=repo_root, branch="main", is_root=True), WorktreeInfo(path=wt_feature, branch="feature", is_root=False), ] } )
# Now you can test assert fake_worktree.find_worktree_for_branch(repo_root, "feature") == wt_feature assert fake_worktree.find_worktree_for_branch(repo_root, "main") == repo_root ```
---
### 2. `WorktreeInfo` Dataclass
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/abc.py` **Lines**: 47-53
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class WorktreeInfo: """Information about a single git worktree."""
path: Path branch: str | None is_root: bool = False ```
**Fields**: - `path: Path` - The filesystem path to the worktree - `branch: str | None` - The branch name checked out in the worktree (None if detached HEAD) - `is_root: bool = False` - Whether this is the root worktree (default is False, only the first worktree in git's list is marked as root)
---
### 3. `FakeGit` Constructor - `worktrees` Parameter
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/fake.py` **Lines**: 116-172 (constructor signature), 176-180 (docstring for worktrees parameter), 238 (initialization)
**Parameter Signature**: ```python def __init__( self, *, worktrees: dict[Path, list[WorktreeInfo]] | None = None, # ... many other parameters ) -> None: ```
**Docstring (from lines 176-180)**: ```python worktrees: Mapping of repo_root -> list of worktrees ```
**How It Works**: ```python from pathlib import Path from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
repo_root = Path("/repo") wt1 = Path("/repo/wt1") wt2 = Path("/repo/wt2")
git_ops = FakeGit( worktrees={ repo_root: [ WorktreeInfo(path=repo_root, branch="main", is_root=True), WorktreeInfo(path=wt1, branch="feature-1", is_root=False), WorktreeInfo(path=wt2, branch="feature-2", is_root=False), ] } )
# Access via the worktree sub-gateway assert len(git_ops.worktree.list_worktrees(repo_root)) == 3 ```
The `worktrees` parameter is passed directly to the internal `FakeWorktree` sub-gateway (lines 339-345): ```python self._worktree_gateway = FakeWorktree( worktrees=self._worktrees, existing_paths=self._existing_paths, dirty_worktrees=self._dirty_worktrees, add_worktree_error=add_worktree_error, remove_worktree_error=self._remove_worktree_error, ) ```
---
### 4. `build_workspace_test_context` Function Signature
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/context_builders.py` **Lines**: 20-81
```python def build_workspace_test_context( env: ErkInMemEnv | ErkIsolatedFsEnv, *, dry_run: bool = False, use_graphite: bool = False, current_branch: str | None = None, **kwargs, ) -> ErkContext: """Build ErkContext for workspace command tests (create, split, consolidate, delete).
This builder provides a standard context configuration for workspace manipulation commands with sensible defaults and support for common testing scenarios.
Args: env: Pure or isolated erk environment fixture dry_run: Whether to wrap git operations with DryRunGit (default: False) use_graphite: Whether to enable Graphite integration (default: False) current_branch: Current branch name for FakeGit configuration (default: None) **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to env.build_context() (can include custom git, github, graphite, shell instances)
Returns: ErkContext configured for workspace command testing
Example: >>> with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: ... ctx = build_workspace_test_context(env, dry_run=True) ... result = runner.invoke(cli, ["delete", "branch"], obj=ctx) """ ```
**Return Type**: `ErkContext`
**Parameters**: - `env: ErkInMemEnv | ErkIsolatedFsEnv` - Environment fixture (pure in-memory or isolated filesystem) - `dry_run: bool = False` - Wrap git with DryRunGit for safety testing - `use_graphite: bool = False` - Enable Graphite integration - `current_branch: str | None = None` - Current branch for FakeGit setup - `**kwargs` - Additional arguments (custom git, github, graphite, shell instances)
**Example Usage**: ```python from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context from click.testing import CliRunner
def test_command() -> None: runner = CliRunner() with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: ctx = build_workspace_test_context( env, dry_run=True, current_branch="feature-branch" )
result = runner.invoke(cli, ["delete", "branch"], obj=ctx) assert result.exit_code == 0 ```
---
### Related Documentation
The testing patterns doc you should read first is: - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/testing.md` - Comprehensive reference on FakeGit setup, mutation tracking, and test patterns - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/test-context-composition.md` - Pattern for shared FakeGitHubIssues instances in contexts
